questions.scenario: A team says, `The API failed on App Service once, so move everything to VMs.` What is the better review response?

options.text: Agree, because VMs are easier to debug for every workload.
First identify whether the failure came from code, settings, identity, health checks, deployment slots, scale, or a true platform limitation.
Move only production to a VM and keep staging on App Service.
Disable health checks so App Service stops blocking traffic.

questions.explanation: A failure on one service does not prove the hosting category is wrong. The team needs evidence before increasing ownership.

questions.scenario: During an incident review, the team finds an old compute decision record that only says `we used the default`. They want a record that would help the next on-call engineer reason about App Service, Container Apps, Functions, or VMs. Which details make that record useful?

options.text: The workload shape and why the selected Azure service matches it.
The owner, resource group, region, health check, logs, identity, and rollback or recovery path.
A statement that the portal default was accepted.
Known limits, such as cold starts, revision traffic, VM maintenance, or app plan capacity.

questions.explanation: The decision record should help the on-call engineer understand why the service exists and how to inspect it.
